beats.headline: They didn't hack the computer. They called the staff.
beats.body: Attackers phoned employees, talked their way into an internal admin tool, and hijacked high-profile accounts. The exploit was a person, not a port.
say: Every gap, every shortcut, every forgotten door — I will find it.
Not tonight. And the easiest door of all is a person who hasn't been trained.
So the people are the perimeter too?
The most-attacked one. Let's defend them.

beats.title: THE HUMAN GATE
say: Walls and towers? Tedious. People are so much easier to fool.
Which is why the strongest controls guard the people, and their data.

beats.title: THE KEEPERS OF THE PACT
beats.subtitle: Training · Personnel · Privacy
say: Layer three is human. Train them, trust them carefully, and honor their data.
Three families keep the pact: Awareness & Training, Personnel Security, and Privacy.

beats.title: THE KEEPERS OF THE PACT
say: AT, PS, and PT. The teacher, the oathkeeper, and the privacy herald.

beats.protects: Trains every citizen to spot a trick at the gate.
beats.reality: Security and privacy awareness plus role-based training so people recognize and resist threats.
say: A trained citizen spots the trick at the gate before it opens.
In reality, that's security and privacy training so people recognize and resist threats like phishing.

beats.plain: A fake message — email, text, or phone call — designed to trick a person into giving up a password or clicking something harmful.
beats.example: An 'urgent' email that looks like IT, asking you to 'verify your password' on a lookalike page.
say: Bait for people. A message that looks legitimate but is a trap — and it's how most breaches actually begin.

beats.title: Literacy Training and Awareness
beats.plain: Give every user security and privacy awareness training — including phishing.
beats.why: People are the most-targeted control surface; awareness is the cheapest strong defense.
say: How do you stop a perfect phishing email?
You train the human. AT-2 builds that instinct in everyone — the instinct that hangs up on the kind of call we opened with.

beats.title: Role-based Training
beats.plain: Give people role-based training for their specific security duties.
beats.why: An admin, a developer, and a clerk face different risks; AT-3 tailors the lesson.
say: And AT-3 trains people for their particular role.

beats.quote: Provide security and privacy literacy training to system users (including managers, senior executives, and contractors):
beats.cite: NIST SP 800-53r5  ·  AT-2 Literacy Training and Awareness
say: Provide security and privacy literacy training to system users (including managers, senior executives, and contractors):

beats.title: Awareness and Training
bullets: AT-2 Literacy Training and Awareness — awareness training for everyone (incl. phishing)
AT-3 Role-based Training — role-based training for specific duties
AT-4 Training Records — keep training records
say: The cheapest firewall you own is an alert human.

beats.oath: The sharpest blade is an alert mind.
say: The Drillmaster's oath. The sharpest blade is an alert mind.

beats.protects: Vets every guard, and reclaims the keys when they leave.
beats.reality: Screening personnel and adjusting access on transfer or termination; insider-risk safeguards.
say: Every guard you hire... could be mine.
In reality, that's screening people, adjusting access when they move or leave, and guarding against insider risk.

beats.title: Personnel Screening
beats.plain: Screen people before granting access — and rescreen as needed.
beats.why: Trust is granted, not assumed; screening is how the citadel vets its guards.
say: PS-3 screens people before they're trusted with access.

beats.title: Personnel Termination
beats.plain: When someone leaves, revoke access and recover assets — promptly.
beats.why: A departing admin who keeps access is a breach waiting to happen.
say: PS-4 reclaims the keys, on the way out the door.

beats.title: Personnel Security
bullets: PS-3 Personnel Screening — screen people before access
PS-4 Personnel Termination — revoke access on termination
PS-5 Personnel Transfer — adjust access on transfer
PS-7 External Personnel Security — hold external workers to the same bar
beats.mnemonic: “Vet them in, and key them out.”
say: Access should follow the person, and leave when they do.

beats.oath: Trust is earned at the gate, and returned at the door.
say: The Oathkeeper's oath. Trust is earned at the gate, and returned at the door.

beats.family_name: Personally Identifiable Information Processing and Transparency
beats.protects: Protects citizens' personal stories, and tells them how they are used.
beats.reality: Lawful, transparent processing of personally identifiable information, with consent and notice.
say: The Privacy Herald, P-T, Processing and Transparency of personal data.
New in Revision 5, privacy now stands beside security.
In reality, that's processing people's personal data lawfully and openly — with notice and consent where required.

beats.plain: Information that can identify a specific person — on its own, or combined with other data.
beats.example: A name with a Social Security number, a home address, a medical record, or an account number.
say: Personally identifiable information — the data that points to a real human being. Privacy is about protecting it, and the person.

beats.title: Authority to Process Personally Identifiable Information
beats.plain: Have lawful authority before processing people's personal information.
beats.why: Privacy starts with a simple question: are we even allowed to do this?
say: PT-2 demands a documented authority to process personal data.

beats.title: Privacy Notice
beats.plain: Tell people, clearly, how their personal information is used.
beats.why: Transparency is the promise behind the pact: no secret uses of personal data.
say: And PT-5 gives people honest notice of how their data is used.

beats.title: The privacy pact, in order
say: Authority first, then purpose and notice, then consent where required, and process only as authorized.
Permission where needed, and honesty, before the data is used.

beats.quote: Determine and document the [authority] that permits the [processing] of personally identifiable information;
beats.cite: NIST SP 800-53r5  ·  PT-2 Authority to Process Personally Identifiable Information
say: Determine and document the [authority] that permits the [processing] of personally identifiable information;

beats.title: Personally Identifiable Information Processing and Transparency
bullets: PT-2 Authority to Process Personally Identifiable Information — have authority to process PII
PT-3 Personally Identifiable Information Processing Purposes — process PII only for stated purposes
PT-4 Consent — get consent where required
PT-5 Privacy Notice — give a clear privacy notice
say: Security protects the data. Privacy honors the person.

beats.oath: By what right, for what purpose, with whose leave?
say: The Privacy Herald's oath. By what right, for what purpose, with whose leave?

beats.title: The Keepers in the RMF
bullets: These are people and program controls, not just technical ones
PT weaves privacy through the whole catalog — new in Revision 5
In practice: train, screen, and process personal data lawfully and openly
say: Not every control is a firewall. Some are a habit, a contract, a promise.

beats.q: A departing administrator still has their access a week later. Which control failed?
say: Scenario: a leaver kept their access. Which guardian dropped the ball?

beats.q: Which family is new in Revision 5 and governs personal-data processing?
say: And which guardian is the newcomer to the catalog?

beats.title: Episode 03 — the Keepers of the Pact
lines: AT trains the human firewall, including phishing (AT-2, AT-3, AT-4).
PS vets people in and keys them out (PS-3, PS-4, PS-5, PS-7).
PT keeps personal data (PII) lawful, honest, and as-promised (PT-2, PT-3, PT-5).
beats.mnemonic: People are the perimeter — train them, screen them, and honor their data.
say: Notebook: not every control is a firewall — some are a habit, a contract, a promise.
Then I'll strike where no one is even looking. Your plans.
Then it's time to meet the High Council.
